tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Energy methods often simplify problems by avoiding time and acceleration details. Is energy conserved in this system? What forms of energy are present (kinetic, gravitational potential, elastic)? Are non-conservative forces (friction) doing work?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Work: W = Fd cos θ. Kinetic energy: KE = ½mv². Gravitational PE: U_g = mgh (near Earth). Elastic PE: U_s = ½kx². Work-energy theorem: W_net = ΔKE. Conservation: KE_i + U_i + W_nc = KE_f + U_f.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: Choose a reference point for h = 0. Identify initial and final states. If friction is present, include W_friction = −f_k × distance as non-conservative work. For springs, include elastic PE at compressed/stretched positions.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Write the energy equation: ½mv₁² + mgh₁ + ½kx₁² + W_nc = ½mv₂² + mgh₂ + ½kx₂². Plug in known values. Solve for the unknown. Alternatively, use W_net = ΔKE if analyzing forces over a displacement directly.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.

tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Work transfers energy. Positive work adds energy to a system; negative work removes it. Where does energy start, and where does it end up in this problem?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Power: P = W/t = Fv. Average power = total work / time. Conservative forces (gravity, spring) allow PE definitions. Non-conservative forces (friction) dissipate mechanical energy as thermal energy.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: For roller coaster or ramp problems, set h = 0 at the lowest convenient point. At the top of a frictionless track, KE ≈ 0 and E = mgh. If friction acts over distance d, subtract μmgd from the initial energy.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Calculate initial total mechanical energy. Subtract work done by friction if applicable. Set equal to final KE + final PE. Example: mgh − μmgd = ½mv². Cancel mass if possible. Verify energy units (Joules).

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.
